headline: French 'libertines' rapes case: From livestreams to camp sites, where the sexual crimes took place
description: Christophe B. and many other men he recruited are accused of raping women, with these sexual assaults broadcast online and in public. Responsibility is now being called into question. This is the final part of our investigation into the French 'libertines' rapes case.

Trump to impose 50% tariffs on many Canadian goods
description: The US president is claiming 'discriminatory treatment' against American alcohol, automobile and dairy products. The tariffs will take effect in 30 days.

headline: In southern Lebanon, at the Secours populaire hospital in Nabatieh, caregivers strive to overcome the traumas of war
description: Doctors Without Borders provides individual counseling and group therapy sessions to professionals who have endured four months of conflict and an ongoing, unstable security situation.

headline: Climate change: Why the ECB refuses to create a lower interest rate for green investments
description: China's central bank guarantees low-cost funding for green investments. Its European counterpart, although a frontrunner on climate change, currently refuses to use this tool.

headline: Cleanliness in Paris: Study reveals 1,412 spots left systematically unclean
description: Paris City Hall turned to architects and municipal cleaning workers to identify 1,412 'black spots,' small public spaces that are systematically soiled or clogged with bulky waste, which it aims to reduce to 1,000.

headline: BTS electrifies Stade de France, seven years after their last Paris concert
description: The world tour of the industry's most powerful K-pop group wrapped up its European leg in Saint-Denis on July 17 and July 18.

headline: Alexander Skarsgård, a daring actor
description: The 49-year-old Swedish actor plays a gay, dominant biker in 'Pillion.' It's an unexpected role for Skarsgård, who is known for Hollywood blockbusters but is determined not to be typecast as a conventional heartthrob.
